subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 29
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known for having a long neck and living in the African savanna?
questions.explanation: Giraffes have incredibly long necks to reach high tree branches to feed on leaves.
questions.hint: It is the tallest animal on land.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their physical traits.

questions.q: What is the capital city of France?
questions.explanation: Paris is the capital of France, famous for landmarks like the Eiffel Tower.
questions.hint: It is called the 'City of Light'.

questions.q: Which part of the plant holds the plant upright?
questions.explanation: The stem provides structural support and transports water and nutrients throughout the plant.
questions.hint: It is like the 'spine' of the plant.

questions.q: What do we call a scientist who studies space?
questions.explanation: An astronomer is someone who studies celestial bodies like stars, planets, and galaxies.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit for your body?
options: Eating a variety of fruits and vegetables
questions.answer: Eating a variety of fruits and vegetables
questions.explanation: Eating colorful fruits and vegetables provides essential vitamins and minerals that keep you healthy.
questions.hint: These are foods grown in nature.

questions.q: Who is the community helper that helps keep our teeth healthy?
questions.explanation: A dentist is a healthcare professional who specializes in oral hygiene and treating tooth problems.
questions.hint: They use special tools to clean your teeth.

questions.q: What is the name of our home galaxy?
questions.explanation: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our entire solar system.
questions.hint: It sounds like a popular chocolate bar.

questions.q: Which of these is a form of energy from the Sun?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is energy captured from the Sun's light and heat.

questions.q: What is the freezing point of water in degrees Celsius?
questions.explanation: Water turns from liquid into solid ice at 0 degrees Celsius.
questions.hint: It is the starting number for the Celsius scale.

questions.q: Which season is known for falling leaves?
questions.explanation: Autumn is the season when trees lose their leaves as the weather gets cooler.
